literal: thought-organ; from √man 'to think' (cognate with Latin *mens*, Greek *menos*, English 'mind')
invariant_definition: The coordinating and doubting (*saṅkalpa-vikalpa*) function of the *antaḥkaraṇa*. *Manas* receives the data of the senses and presents them to the *buddhi* for determination; it is the locus of imagination, doubt, and indeterminate cognition. In Nyāya–Vaiśeṣika *manas* is also a substance (*dravya*) — the internal sense-organ atomic in size that conveys cognitions to the *ātman*. All Vedāntic schools accept *manas* as one of the *antaḥkaraṇa*'s four functions and as instrumental, not constitutive of consciousness — the *ātman* is the ultimate cognizer; *manas* is one of its instruments.

per_school.definition: All accept *manas* as the *saṅkalpa-vikalpa* function and as an instrument of the *ātman*; differences in the broader status of *antaḥkaraṇa* (see *antaḥkaraṇa*) carry over here.

translator_note: 'Mind' is the conventional gloss but is too broad. *Manas* is one specific function within the *antaḥkaraṇa*, not the entire mental life. 'Coordinating function' or 'lower mind' (in some yogic translations) are clearer.
